economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted operations since March 2020 with office closures, travel restrictions and increased compliance costs. First Nation received additional ISC COVID-19 funding. Funding from Dakota Dunes CDC and First Nations Trust was reduced due to SIGA casino closures but returned to pre-pandemic levels in 2022-23. First Nation is party to Touchwood Agency Specific Claim related to alleged mismanagement of agency funds 1920-1924. Consolidated entities include KFN Market Husky, Kawacatoose Enterprises, Kawacatoose General Store Limited Partnership, Kawacatoose Business Development Corporation, and Asiniw Resources Ltd.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022 but audit signed June 19, 2023 — over a year delay. Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). Six entities fully consolidated including KFN Market Husky, Kawacatoose Enterprises, Kawacatoose General Store LP, Business Development Corp, Asiniw Resources Ltd, and CMHC Housing. Kawacatoose Treaty Land Entitlement Trust and Kawacatoose Specific Claim Trust excluded as Chief and Council do not control them. CMHC reserves underfunded by $319,274. First Nation has contingent liability related to Touchwood Agency Specific Claim loan (~$2.25M). Long-term debt includes capital lease obligations of $89,080. Annual debt service calculated as repayment of long-term debt $409,196 plus capital lease repayments $159,111 = $568,307. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $16,973,282 + CMHC $519,457 = $17,492,739; OSR includes retail sales, Touchwood Agency TC, BHP Billiton, First Nations Trust, other, Touchwood Child & Family, CMHC rental, Dakota Dunes CDC, lease income, interest. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. Community Based Services expenses $2,598,136 and Capital $1,567,736 and Post Secondary $1,544,435 and Lands and Membership $534,957 and Band Project Revenue $5,304,027 not mapped to standard categories.
